content:
Apple TV picked up two wins at the Peabody Awards this year, out of five total nominations. Here are the details.

Apple TV scores two wins from five nominations

Apple scored two nods at the 2026 Peabody Awards, which will hold the winners’ ceremony on May 31, in Beverly Hills.

Among the winning documentaries, Ryan White’s Come See Me in the Good Light was lauded alongside HBO Max’s The Alabama Solution, Thoughts & Prayers, and Pee-wee as Himself, BBC Four’s Mr. Nobody Against Putin, Kino Film Collection’s Put Your Soul on Your Hand and Walk, ESPN’s Southpaw—The Life and Legacy of Jim Abbott, and No Other Land.

In the Entertainment category, Apple TV scored a win with Pluribus, alongside 10 other series, including HBO Max’s The Pitt and Heated Rivalry, Adult Swim’s Common Side Effects, and Disney+’s Andor.

This year’s Peabody Awards list includes 34 winners among 68 nominees, which in Apple TV’s case also included Shape Island in the Children’s/Youth category, as well as Vietnam: The War That Changed America, and Mr. Scorsese in the Documentaries category.

Apple TV is available for $12.99 per month and features hit TV shows and movies like Severance, The Studio, The Morning Show, Shrinking and Silo.
